reasoning: While the LLM fidelity analysis found no content or visual issues (scoring 1.0), programmatic detectors identified three MAJOR structural and accessibility defects. These include a redundant full-page image alongside extracted text, a list broken by a page break, and incomplete alt text. Per the count-based routing rules, the presence of 3 major defects requires HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p2→p3: list item ends “…l ash were a concern. Coal ash should be”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...6 memorandum from the North Carolina Department of"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mi
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

action_items: Remove the redundant full-page image if it duplicates extracted text
Merge the split list items across the page break for proper reading order
Update the incomplete alt text to accurately describe the image content
Review and remove residual header/footer text from the HTML
